theorem_statement: If a and b are nonnegative, then a + b is nonnegative.

nodes.title: Nonnegative sum
nodes.informal_statement: If 0 <= a and 0 <= b, then 0 <= a + b.
nodes.informal_proof_text: Assume 0 <= a and 0 <= b. Applying the standard arithmetic lemma that sums of nonnegative reals are nonnegative yields 0 <= a + b.

nodes.title: Sum of nonnegative reals is nonnegative
nodes.informal_statement: If x and y are nonnegative real numbers, then x + y is nonnegative.
nodes.informal_proof_text: This is the standard arithmetic lemma invoked in the proof.

edges.label: applies
edges.explanation: The theorem follows directly by instantiating the standard lemma with x = a and y = b.
